overview: The Marathon County Board of Supervisors held an adjourned organizational meeting where they approved committee appointments, elected Supervisors Hapa and Arstead to the Executive Committee at-large positions, and unanimously approved a resolution to reduce landfill tipping fees for disaster-related debris following severe weather that devastated parts of the county, particularly the Town of Ringle. The board also approved several budget-related resolutions on the consent agenda.

agenda.item: Pledge of Allegiance and moment of reflection
Election of at-large Executive Committee members
Resolution 24-26: Out of home placement reserve increase

discussions.body: Chair Gibbs presented committee appointments developed with Vice Chair Boots, emphasizing goals of increasing diverse perspectives, developing new supervisors as leaders, and honoring supervisor preferences. He noted 34% of the board is in their first or second term. The motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Crims, included both county board chair and administrator appointments. The motion carried but was not unanimous.

discussions.item: Election of At-Large Executive Committee Members
discussions.body: Three supervisors were nominated: Supervisor Hapa (nominated by Supervisor Mask), Supervisor Leur (nominated by Supervisor Rosenberg), and Supervisor Arstead (nominated by Vice Chair Boots). Supervisor Rosenberg explained her nomination of Supervisor Leur, citing her advocacy for the county's youngest children and oldest elders. Paper ballots were used for the vote. Supervisors Hapa and Arstead were elected to the Executive Committee.

Administrator Leonard and Solid Waste Director Hagen Busher presented a proposal to reduce tipping fees for disaster debris following severe weather that impacted Ringle, Kronenwetter, and other communities. The state is waiving $13.10 per ton, Ringle is waiving $2.33, and the county proposes reducing fees to $36.94 per ton (covering costs only). Two residents, Jessica Toasty and Matt Haybeck, were granted special permission to speak, requesting the county waive fees entirely for affected homeowners. Extensive board discussion addressed insurance reimbursement, timeline concerns, and potential community foundation partnerships. The board first voted unanimously to suspend rules to hear the resolution directly, then passed Resolution 26-26 unanimously.

The consent agenda (items C10-C14A) was approved unanimously on motion by Supervisor Cavelli, seconded by Supervisor Drebeck. Resolution 24-26 for out of home placement reserve increase passed but was not unanimous, with motion by Supervisor Morash and second by Supervisor Robinson. Resolution 25-26 to amend the social services budget also passed but was not unanimous.

publicComment: Two residents were granted special permission to speak on the landfill tipping fee resolution. Jessica Toasty requested Marathon County waive all fees for six months, noting that the state, DNR, and Town of Ringle were all waiving their fees, and emphasized that residents are dealing with insurance deductibles and uncovered expenses. Matt Haybeck spoke on behalf of residents whose homes must be demolished, requesting fee waivers for underinsured homeowners and noting that some insurance adjusters haven't yet arrived. He mentioned approximately 44 homes were affected.
actionItems: Reduced landfill tipping fees to $36.94 per ton for certified disaster-related debris through June 17, 2025
Administrator Leonard to continue discussions with Community Foundation about potential reimbursement mechanism for unreimbursed disposal costs
Solid waste department to develop clear long-term policy on handling similar disaster situations within six months
Supervisors Hapa and Arstead appointed to Executive Committee at-large positions
Committee appointments approved for the new term
title: Marathon County Regular Meeting
